pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh which writes output to this exact path.
Task description simulates the role of an Environmental Scientist, which matches standard SOC title.
Matches standard occupational classifications (SOC) for Environmental Scientists.
GDP contribution of a specific occupational title is not a standard tracked economic metric and could not be verified in authoritative sources like BLS or BEA.
Task logic involves injecting impossible values and stuck sensors (Error Injection), and estimating values using Haversine nearest-neighbors (Spatial Analytics).
Task requires LAG/LEAD (Window Functions), SIN/COS/ACOS (PL/SQL Math), QUALITY_CONTROLLED_OBS_MV (Materialized Views), and STDDEV (Statistical Functions).

pi_items.metadata_value: USW00090001 TMAX stuck at 222 for 45 days
pi_items.verified_value: USW00090001 TMAX stuck at 222 for 45 days
pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh PL/SQL generator: Station i=1 (USW00090001) has v_tmax := 222 for d BETWEEN 150 AND 194 (45 days).

pi_items.metadata_value: Various TMIN > TMAX and out-of-bounds temps across stations
pi_items.verified_value: Various TMIN > TMAX and out-of-bounds temps across stations
pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh: v_tmax = -950 (out of bounds) at d*i % 257 = 0, and v_tmin = v_tmax + 50 (TMIN > TMAX) at d*i % 311 = 0.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated weather observations for 30 stations over one year. Setup scripts inject deliberate errors: a stuck TMAX sensor for station USW00090001 reporting 222 for 45 days (days 150-194), physically impossible temperatures (e.g., TMAX = -950), and instances where TMIN exceeds TMAX. The required outputs mandate the use of Haversine spatial interpolation and Oracle statistical functions like STDDEV.
